home.title: SnapBack – Ship faster without fearing your last refactor

content.headline:
SnapBack Learns.
You Experiment.
content.subheadline: Every time you undo a mistake, SnapBack learns what patterns caused it. Day 1: catches common patterns. Day 30: knows YOUR codebase. Month 3: catches what you didn't know to look for.
content.founder_story: Local-first snapshots, cloud-optional learning. Metadata only—your code stays on your machine. Works offline, no account required.
content.trust_line: Local-first · Learns over time · Privacy first
content.video_caption: [TODO: 60-second hero video] Real SnapBack session showing Intelligent Snapshots learning: snapshot after AI edit → Restore triggered → SnapBack learns what broke

content.label: The learning loop
content.headline: Intelligent Snapshots Gets Smarter
content.body: Copilot, Claude, Cursor—they move fast. Great ideas. But some refactors break your specific patterns. SnapBack watches what goes wrong and learns what to flag next time. Your codebase is unique. SnapBack's intelligent snapshots becomes unique too.

timeline_items.title: Day 1: Catches common patterns out of the box
timeline_items.description: Install SnapBack. It detects hardcoded secrets, phantom dependencies, test code in production, and risky patterns right away.

timeline_items.title: Day 30: Learns YOUR specific risks
timeline_items.description: Every time you restore, SnapBack learns. 'Cursor always breaks this auth module.' 'This refactor pattern cascades.' 'This folder has phantom dependency issues.' Accuracy improves with your usage.

timeline_items.title: Month 3: Catches patterns before you do
timeline_items.description: After 90 days, intelligent snapshots knows what breaks in YOUR codebase. Flags issues before you'd even think to look. Becomes predictive for your patterns.

content.closing: That's intelligent snapshots. It's not magic—it's outcome learning. Every undo teaches it something.

steps.title: Automatic snapshots
steps.body: Every save creates a restore point. Local-first, no setup. SnapBack watches your edits and remembers the state of your workspace.

steps.title: Detects what breaks
steps.body: Intelligent snapshots flags hardcoded secrets, phantom dependencies, test code in production, and risky patterns. Learns which issues matter most for YOUR code.

content.label: Session timeline
content.headline: See your coding session as a timeline.
content.description: Every save becomes a snapshot on your session timeline. Scroll back through your AI-assisted session, inspect diffs, and restore any point where things still worked.

content.label: Intelligent snapshots gets smarter
content.headline: Fast. Private. Learning.
content.body: SnapBack works locally, learns progressively, and keeps your code private. Snapshots happen in the background. Restores are instant. Intelligent snapshots improves with every outcome.

metric_cards.title: <200ms snapshots
metric_cards.description: No editor lag. Intelligent snapshots captures your workspace state while you keep coding.

metric_cards.title: Instant restores
metric_cards.description: One click gets you back to the last safe state. Works on monorepos, microservices, and large codebases.

metric_cards.title: Code stays local
metric_cards.description: Snapshots live in ~/.snapback. Metadata-only for cloud features. Your code never leaves your machine unless you choose to share it.

content.label: Your code, your control
content.headline: Intelligent Snapshots stays local. Learning is optional.

items.title: Snapshots never leave your machine by default.
items.description: Every snapshot stores locally in ~/.snapback. Zero cloud uploads. Works offline. No third-party storage or tracking. Verify it yourself: ls -la ~/.snapback.

items.title: Intelligent snapshots learns from outcomes, not source code.
items.description: Cloud features send metadata only: 'file changed,' 'test failed,' 'restored here.' Never your actual code. You opt-in to learning features.

items.title: No account required to get protection.
items.description: Install SnapBack and start creating snapshots immediately. Completely offline. Intelligent snapshots works in local-only mode—decide later if you want cloud learning.

content.footer: Your code is your asset. SnapBack is transparent about what gets stored and learned.

content.headline: SnapBack learns. Let it protect you.
content.body: Start with local snapshots today. Intelligent snapshots learns instantly. No account needed. Works offline. Metadata-only cloud features. Your code stays on your machine—decide later if you want cloud learning and pattern intelligence across your team.

content.headline: How SnapBack keeps you safe while you code.
content.body:
SnapBack is a local-first timeline of your development session.
It creates smart snapshots of your workspace at meaningful moments and gives you a fast, precise way to roll back when something goes sideways.

content.headline: Smart snapshots, not noisy backups.
content.body: SnapBack is mostly invisible. It only snaps when you reach a meaningful milestone.

triggers.title: File saves
triggers.description: Background snapshots on every save so you never lose flow.

triggers.title: Integration Tests
triggers.description: Snap before running full suites. Restore instantly if they fail.

triggers.title: Context Switches
triggers.description: Changing branches or pulling main triggers a safety snapshot.

triggers.title: AI Agent Actions
triggers.description: Automatically snapshots before Claude/Cursor applies large diffs.

content.headline: Your whole session, as a scrollable timeline.
content.body: SnapBack clusters snapshots into sessions — “Today’s spike”, “API refactor”, “Bug hunt”. Each session keeps your moments in order, so you can scrub back to the one that feels right instead of guessing.
highlights: Semantic labels – “Before auth refactor”, not just “12:03 PM”.
Filters – Show only snapshots linked to test runs or a specific file.
Search – Type “refactor” to jump to moments you deliberately marked.

tabs.label: VS Code
tabs.title: Command palette first
tabs.body: Hit `⌘⇧P` / `Ctrl+Shift+P`, type “SnapBack: Restore Snapshot”, pick the moment, and confirm. Your workspace rolls back in one atomic operation.

tabs.label: CLI
tabs.title: Terminal-friendly
tabs.body: Use `snapback restore --snapshot <id>` to integrate with your own scripts and workflows.

tabs.label: API
tabs.title: Automation hooks
tabs.body: Coming soon: programmatic access for custom tooling, bots, and CI helpers.

content.headline: Under the hood: boring on purpose.
content.body: SnapBack uses a local SQLite datastore to track snapshots and file states. Restores are applied via a cautious engine that checks for conflicts and never touches untracked files.
bullets: Atomic restores – Either the entire operation succeeds, or nothing changes.
Conflict awareness – Warns you if a restore would overwrite newer work.
Audit trail – You can see what changed for every restore operation.

content.headline: What’s coming next
items: Smarter labels powered by local AI context

content.headline: Simple pricing, no tricks.
content.body: Start free as a solo dev. Upgrade when you want to roll it out to your team.

plans.name: Solo
plans.description: For individual developers who want a personal safety net.

plans.name: Pro
plans.description: For people who live in their editor and ship daily.

plans.name: Team
plans.description: For teams that want SnapBack as standard dev tooling.

faqs.question: Will SnapBack upload my source code?
faqs.answer: No. Snapshots stay local. We only use cloud services for auth, updates, and anonymous telemetry you explicitly opt into.

faqs.question: Is this a replacement for git?
faqs.answer: No. SnapBack sits underneath git and your editor. It’s about sessions and workspace state, not commit history.

faqs.question: Does SnapBack slow my editor down?
faqs.answer: We designed it to be invisible until you need it. Snapshotting is lightweight and tuned for active development.

faqs.question: What happens if a restore goes wrong?
faqs.answer: Every restore is tracked and reversible. If something looks off, you can revert the restore itself.

content.headline: Get SnapBack running in under 5 minutes.
content.body: Follow this quick path to install the extension, run your first experiment, and feel the safety net kick in.

steps.title: Install the VS Code extension
steps.body: Search for “SnapBack” in the VS Code marketplace, or click the button below.

steps.title: Connect your account (optional at first)
steps.body: Use GitHub / Google / email to link your account for sync and updates.

steps.title: Run a test refactor
steps.body: Change something a bit scary in a throwaway file. Then use SnapBack to roll back and feel the safety net kick in.

content.headline: Your first restore, step-by-step.

steps.title: Make a change
steps.body: Edit a file, run your tests, or let AI rewrite a chunk of code.

steps.title: Pick the moment
steps.body: Choose the snapshot labeled “Before refactor”. Confirm. Watch your workspace roll back.

content.closing: That’s it. From here, SnapBack quietly watches your back while you code.

content.headline: Make SnapBack part of your muscle memory.

tips.title: Name important snapshots
tips.body: Add little notes like “known-good” to make them easy to find.

tips.title: Restore early, not late
tips.body: If something feels off, jump back quickly instead of thrashing.

tips.title: Use it on spikes
tips.body: Treat experiments as cheap because getting back is cheap.

content.headline: Help shape SnapBack.
content.body: We’re onboarding early users slowly so we can ship the right things. Tell us how you work and we’ll invite you as we expand.
